 - OpenAI will launch GPT-5.6, its most advanced AI model, on Thursday after a delay prompted by U.S. government concerns over the potential national security risks posed by increasingly powerful AI systems.

The release comes amid ​intensifying competition among AI developers who are racing to improve model performance, cut costs and ‌expand capabilities for enterprise customers, fueling a wave of new systems and reasoning models across the industry.

Make sense of global markets with the Trading Day newsletter. Sign up here.

Meanwhile, Chinese developers are reshaping the economics of AI by delivering increasingly capable models at a fraction of the cost. [...] xAI SuperGrok.$30/month.Grok 4, ​image and video generation, connectors, expert tools and higher usage limits.
Meta Meta AI.Free.Helps draft text, summarize documents, brainstorm and ​create images across WhatsApp, Facebook and Instagram.
Mistral Le Chat Pro.Pro plan at $14.99/month.Runs on the Mistral Medium 3.5 model, with higher usage ‌limits, ⁠advanced coding, complex task handling, more image generation and priority support. [...] Reporting by Anhata Rooprai in Bengaluru; Editing by Pooja Desai

items.title: TJ Newsflash 08 July – AI, overwork, sleep and freelance friction reshape work - Training Journal
items.summary:
Read more.

### AI agents are not your “coworkers”

Emma Wiles, a Boston University business profess
items.content:
Read more.

### AI agents are not your “coworkers”

Emma Wiles, a Boston University business professor, found treating AI as a “coworker” and not a software tool would lead you to do a worse job. Wiles found that people caught 18% fewer errors when the work was said to have come from an agentic “AI employee” rather than a chatbot.

Read more.

### Skills England has published its first Annual Skills Report

Skills England has published its first Annual Skills Report, providing the most comprehensive picture to date of current and future skills demand across England. It sets out actions to tackle key challenges and build a more responsive, high-quality skills system. [...] Skip to content

# TJ Newsflash 08 July – AI, overwork, sleep and freelance friction reshape work

 Jo Cook
 5 mins
 News

## The latest L&D news, reports, research and updates, personally compiled by TJ’s Editor, Jo Cook. This week: Gen Z office design woes, widening cyber preparedness gaps, why humanising AI hurts performance, Skills England’s stark talent forecast, and positive evidence that brain health can improve at any age for adults everywhere.

### UK tech professionals shift learning towards AI as coding skills evolve, according to new O’Reilly data [...] New research from O’Reilly, a leading source for insight-driven learning on technology and business, suggests that UK tech professionals are building on existing programming expertise to adopt new AI-powered tools and ways of learning.

Analysis of O’Reilly platform usage between Q1 2025 and Q1 2026 shows Generative AI content usage grew 89% in the UK. AI and Machine Learning as a whole grew 51% in the UK, while Data now accounts for 31% of all UK platform usage, making it the largest topic on the platform.

Read more.

### Always-on work exposes the gap between modern workforce demands and outdated management models

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market prof
items.content:
Skip to main content

Report AdImage 1

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv

 - The U.S. Department of Commerce has approved a broad launch of OpenAI's advanced GPT-5.6 model following ​additional government testing under Washington's new oversight framework for frontier ‌artificial intelligence, Axios reported on Tuesday.

OpenAI expects to release GPT 5.6 more widely this week following additional testing and meetings with U.S. government officials, the report said, citing a person ​familiar with the matter.

Learn about the latest breakthroughs in AI and tech with the Reuters Artificial Intelligencer newsletter. Sign up here.

Advertisement · Scroll to continue

Image 3Video 1

Report AdImage 4 [...] Washington has increased scrutiny of advanced model releases ‌to ⁠identify potential threats on concerns that technology could be misused by military or intelligence in China, Russia or other countries of concern.

Advertisement · Scroll to continue

The delay followed a June executive order by U.S. President Donald Trump establishing ​a voluntary framework under which AI ​developers can provide "covered ⁠frontier models" to the U.S. government for up to 30 days before releasing them to trusted partners.

Anthropic said ​last week that the Commerce Department lifted restrictions ​on access ⁠to its most advanced Fable and Mythos AI models, less than three weeks after ordering the company to suspend availability of the systems ⁠over ​national security risks.

items.title: AI startups are breaking revenue growth records - Zamin.uz
items.summary: It is not just the developers of large models that are growing rapidly, but also services specialize
items.content:
It is not just the developers of large models that are growing rapidly, but also services specialized in specific areas. For instance, the startup Sierra, which creates AI agents for corporate clients, reached its first $100 million ARR in seven quarters, but it took only two quarters to add the next $100 million. Glean has seen a similar situation: it took nine months to grow revenue from $100 million to $200 million, while the $300 million milestone was conquered in just six months.

## Integration of traditional business with AI

items.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face bring new models and frameworks to LeRobot - The Robot Report
items.summary: Isaac GR00T 1.7 is an open, reasoning vision-language-action (VLA) model for humanoid robots. Togeth
items.content:
Isaac GR00T 1.7 is an open, reasoning vision-language-action (VLA) model for humanoid robots. Together, these integrations give developers a more accessible and standardized path for end-to-end robot development while driving innovation and collaboration across the open robotics community.

Open-source AI has shown how quickly developers can innovate when models, data, and tools are shared, said the partners. Robotics has the same opportunity, but advancements in physical AI development can still be gated by costly and fragmented resources, from large datasets and robot foundation models to simulation, compute, and validation tools. [...] “Open source is how a field turns advanced research into something people can study, adapt and build on,” said Thomas Wolf, co-founder and chief science officer at Hugging Face. “With NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7 and Isaac TeleOp in LeRobot today, robotics developers can use shared models, data, and workflows to train and evaluate robots in the open. And with NVIDIA Cosmos 3 planned next, the community will have a path to bring frontier world models into that same collaborative loop.”

## NVIDIA, Hugging Face connect robotics developers with AI builders [...] Hugging Face LeRobot is an open-source robotics library for training, running, and sharing robot datasets, models, policies, and workflows. NVIDIA’s continued partnership with the company connects NVIDIA’s 3 million robotics developers with Hugging Face’s 16 million AI builders, expanding access to frontier physical AI tools through open workflows.

Bringing NVIDIA physical AI capabilities into LeRobot gives developers a common way to collect and standardize data, train and fine-tune robot foundation models, evaluate performance, and deploy models through open workflows.

The integrations include:

53. Botvinick, M., Wang, J. X., Dabney, W., Miller, K. J. & Kurth-Nelson, Z. Deep reinforcement learning and its neuroscientific implications. Neuron 107, 603–616 (2020). [...] ### Box 1: an example AI framework

To illustrate how the proposed framework can be implemented in practice, consider a hypothetical but realistic EEG dataset in which participants perform a parametrically varying conflict-monitoring task (e.g., a continuous Flanker or Stroop paradigm). The dataset comprises trial-wise EEG time-frequency decompositions, single-trial behavioral indices (reaction time and accuracy), and metadata regarding trial context (conflict level, expectancy, and previous-trial history). [...] resulting latent space is structured to potentially capture low-dimensional patterns within the observed data. However, the outcome and suitability of the low-dimensional representation depend on the nature of the data. Within our proposed framework, we conceptualize these latent dynamics as the underlying cognitive state dynamics (or the subspace that cognitive processes may exploit to operate).

items.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips - TechCrunch
items.summary: It also helped that this small team is well funded for its size. Thanks to his track record as VP of
items.content:
It also helped that this small team is well funded for its size. Thanks to his track record as VP of engineering of Zenly, which Snapchat acquired for nine figures in 2017, Morin raised $20 million from venture firms including Harry Stebbings’ 20VC, >commit, AALVC, Drysdale Ventures, Xavier Niel’s Kima Ventures, Kindred Capital, LocalGlobe, and Puzzle Ventures.

Unlike ZML’s first public project, the inference-focused ML framework released in 2024 and updated in March, ZML/LLMD is not open source. But it is launching as a free product with the goal of learning about usage. “I’d rather measure and [then generate revenue] where it is most effective without hindering my growth stupidly because I have been too greedy from the get-go,” Morin said.

items.title: The Key-Value Cache is the Missing Layer in Overcoming the AI Memory Wall - Automation World
items.summary:
This is a good thing.

Deploying that infrastructure, however, exposes an architectural reality that
items.content:
This is a good thing.

Deploying that infrastructure, however, exposes an architectural reality that training clusters largely sidestepped: the memory wall. Model training is compute-bound and happily consumes every FLOP a GPU can deliver. Inference is different. Its auto-regressive, token-by-token generation is fundamentally memory-bound. AI infrastructure needs extreme memory bandwidth to feed the compute cores, but it also needs massive memory capacity. Modern systems must hold 70-billion- or even 400-billion-parameter models in active memory while simultaneously managing enterprise contexts that routinely stretch from 8,000 to well over 100,000 tokens. [...] Control"},"section\_hierarchy":[{"id":33182,"name":"Factory","alias":"factory"},{"id":63681,"name":"Control","alias":"control"}],"taxonomy":[{"type":"Tag","name":"AI infrastructure","fullName":"Tag: AI infrastructure (4209849)","id":4209849},{"type":"Tag","name":"predictive maintenance","fullName":"Tag: predictive maintenance (4083062)","id":4083062},{"type":"Tag","name":"artificial intelligence","fullName":"Tag: artificial intelligence (3150015)","id":3150015},{"type":"Tag","name":"machine learning","fullName":"Tag: machine learning (3142382)","id":3142382},{"type":"Tag","name":"data","fullName":"Tag: data (3136038)","id":3136038}],"authors":[{"id":55389362,"name":"Torry Steed"}]}" name="BlueConic">

Control

# The Key-Value Cache is the Missing Layer in Overcoming the AI Memory Wall [...] Today, the operational reality is pivoting sharply toward inference. As AI and machine learning become central to the automation space, powering robotics, predictive maintenance, and real-time quality control, enterprises are moving AI out of the lab and into production. They are bringing it onto factory floors and out in the field, demanding on-premises infrastructure that delivers strict data sovereignty, predictable latency, and tightly controlled costs.

This is a good thing.

items.title: Biohub Identifies Psoriasis Targets Using AI - Let's Data Science
items.summary:
## Scoring Rationale

The study is notable because it combines genome-wide CRISPR in primary human s
items.content:
## Scoring Rationale

The study is notable because it combines genome-wide CRISPR in primary human skin cells with AI-assisted prioritization and in vivo validation. It remains a translational research advance rather than a clinical treatment change, so the score stays in the notable range.

MoreHealthcare AI news→

## Sources

Public references used for this report.

4 sources

Back to News FeedNews archive [...] ### Technical context

The study is a useful example of AI-assisted target prioritization rather than autonomous drug discovery. The model helped surface candidates, but the evidentiary weight comes from the genome-wide perturbation screen, orthogonal validation and mouse-model results. That distinction matters because weak or biased screening data would still produce weak AI-assisted hypotheses.

### For practitioners

Discovery teams should treat AI ranking as one layer in a reproducible experimental stack. Useful controls include guide-level repr

items.title: Google just wiped out 50,000 AI slop networks. Marketers, pay attention - The Drum
items.summary:
## Platforms are looking at patterns, not just pages

Google’s paper focuses on online video platfor
items.content:
## Platforms are looking at patterns, not just pages

Google’s paper focuses on online video platforms rather than organic search results, so it would be a mistake to treat it as a direct ranking-system leak. But the direction of travel is still important for anyone involved in search, content, PR or brand marketing.

The paper describes a system designed to identify clusters of coordinated accounts showing patterns of synthetic content. It looks at signals such as account-relatedness, upload pacing, repeated narratives, synthetic media patterns and behavioral similarities across channels.

That’s a much bigger idea than simply asking whether one blog post, video or caption was created with generative AI.

It suggests platforms are becoming more interested in behavior than authorship.

items.title: Google Cuts Surface Code Error Rate To 7.72 × 10−4 With RL - Quantum Zeitgeist
items.summary: The pursuit of stable quantum computation took a significant step forward as researchers demonstrate
items.content: The pursuit of stable quantum computation took a significant step forward as researchers demonstrated a reinforcement learning agent capable of actively counteracting environmental drift in a superconducting processor, paving the way for sustained, error-corrected calculations. Experiments conducted on a Willow superconducting processor achieved average logical error rates of 7.72 × 10−4 and 8.19 × 10−3. The system manages over a thousand control parameters, which dictate how abstract quantum error correction circuits translate into physical control waveforms. The agent’s ability to reach high performance even when initialized with randomized control parameters suggests a potential replacement for traditional calibration methods. [...] A logical error rate of 7.72 × 10−4 achieved with the surface code demonstrates a significant advance toward stable quantum computation. Researchers at Google Quantum AI and Google DeepMind unified the processes of calibration and computation to reach this milestone. The core innovation lies in giving the quantum error correction process a dual role: not only correcting the quantum state, but also teaching a reinforcement learning agent to stabilize the system. This framework was experimentally demonstrated on a Willow superconducting processor, improving the logical stability of the surface code 3.5-fold with complementary decoder steering. Beyond surface codes, the team also achieved an average logical error rate of 8.19 × 10−3 with the color code. The reinforcement learning agent [...] Google Quantum AI researchers report achieving a logical error rate of 7.72 × 10−4 using the surface code, a crucial step toward stable quantum computation. The team unified calibration with computation by repurposing quantum error detection events, typically used for correction, as a learning signal for a reinforcement learning agent. This allows the quantum computer to continuously adjust its control parameters during computation, improving the log

items.title: Google just wiped out 50,000 AI slop networks. Marketers, pay attention - The Drum
items.summary:
## The weakest AI strategies are volume-led

The brands most at risk are not the ones using AI. They
items.content:
## The weakest AI strategies are volume-led

The brands most at risk are not the ones using AI. They’re the ones using AI to replace thinking.

The ones publishing content because a keyword exists, not because they have something useful to say. The ones building page after page of near-identical copy with local modifiers swapped in. The ones treating search visibility as a production-volume game rather than a trust-building exercise.

That approach was already weak. AI has just made it faster.

There is also a brand safety angle here. AI slop is not only an SEO issue. It affects paid media, social platforms, creator ecosystems, online reviews, affiliate content and digital PR. [...] The useful lesson from Google’s research is that platforms are not just trying to detect synthetic media. They’re trying to detect synthetic systems. That’s a very different challenge, and it should change how marketers think about content strategy.

## AI can support the workflow, but it cannot be the strategy

The safest route is not to pretend that AI isn’t part of the workflow. That would be unrealistic.

The safer route is to make sure AI is not the only thing in the workflow.

Strong content still needs subject expertise, editorial judgment, original insight, real examples, clear positioning and a reason to exist.

AI can help with production. It shouldn’t be allowed to become the strategy. [...] For brands, the lesson is not to avoid AI, but to avoid building content systems that look indistinguishable from scaled synthetic spam. As an AI SEO agency, Sierra Six Media helps businesses use automation in ways that strengthen search visibility without sacrificing originality, editorial judgment or long-term trust.

items.title: Meta Launches Muse Image, Enables Instagram Face Reuse - Let's Data Science
items.summary: TechCrunch reports a specific social-media integration: users can tag a public Instagram account so
items.content:
TechCrunch reports a specific social-media integration: users can tag a public Instagram account so the generator can draw on that account's photos to create new AI images. TechCrunch, citing Wired, reproduces two attributed lines from Meta: "people may be able to create content with your Instagram content using AI features at Meta" and "You will not be notified about content created using AI features at Meta." CNET documents a hands-on example in which a reporter used the tool to generate a face-swapped image from a friend's public Instagram photos, and CNET urges that Instagram users who do not want this should change their settings to opt out.

#### Editorial analysis [...] For practitioners, models that can ingest and reapply social-media imagery at scale raise dataset-provenance, moderation, and privacy trade-offs. Reported facts: Meta released an AI image model called Muse Image, built by Meta Superintelligence Labs, and made it available through the Meta AI app, Instagram Stories and WhatsApp, CNBC reports. TechCrunch reports that the tool includes a feature allowing users to tag a public Instagram account and use that account's images as source material for AI-generated images. TechCrunch (citing Wired) reports Meta said, "people may be able to create content with your Instagram content using AI features at Meta" and "You will not be notified about content created using AI features at Meta." CNET and Gizmodo document privacy concerns and show how users [...] MoreMeta AI news→

items.summary: The company’s long-term vision is that intent will become the defining signal of the AI era, similar
items.content:
The company’s long-term vision is that intent will become the defining signal of the AI era, similar to how keywords shaped search engines and identity shaped social networks.

"" [...] At the center of the platform is what Velocity calls “user intent” — the information people reveal when they interact with AI systems about what they want to learn, create, buy or solve. The company says AI conversations provide a more direct signal than traditional advertising methods based on cookies and browsing history, which are increasingly restricted by privacy regulations.

Velocity’s platform includes an AI advertising network, a mediation and auction layer designed to maximize revenue across demand sources, and a conversation intelligence system that turns AI interactions into structured, privacy-safe intent signals.

items.title: A top Morgan Stanley bond manager flags problems brewing in Big Tech's AI debt binge - Business Insider
items.summary: or @jennreports.81 on the encrypted messaging app Signal. She can also be reached on LinkedIn.Story
items.content:
or @jennreports.81 on the encrypted messaging app Signal. She can also be reached on LinkedIn.Story highlights [...] Vishal Khanduja, the head of broad markets fixed income at the bank, flagged worrying signs he sees in the corporate debt market, particularly among the for Big Tech giants, which have been raising billions of dollars in the bond market to pay for their sprawling AI projects.

One concern that seems to be emerging in particular: Big Tech may be borrowing too much, Khanduja said in an interview with Bloomberg TV on Wednesday.

"I think credit risk is too undervalued right now in the market," Khanduja said, pointing to reports this week that Amazon plans to sell $25 billion in bonds. That issuance was a "surprise," he said, given that many investors thought the tech giant was done borrowing for the year. [...] Jennifer Sor is a reporter at Business Insider. She covers financial markets and the economy, with a focus on retail investing, job trends, and the pursuit of wealth. She regularly speaks to famed forecasters and top investors in markets, including Gary Shilling, Nouriel Roubini, and Larry McDonald. Her work has also been featured in outlets such as Forbes, Bloomberg Opinion's "Money Stuff," and SiriusXM Business Radio. Prior to her time at BI, Jennifer covered tech and business news at the San Francisco Chronicle and Los Angeles Business Journal. items.title: Job Hunters Turn to AI Classes for New Skills - GovTech
items.summary: "You can tell if it's AI-written," he said. "If you literally just copy and pasted this, and you did
items.content:
"You can tell if it's AI-written," he said. "If you literally just copy and pasted this, and you didn't spend time to read over what you're submitting or personalizing it, that would be a fail."  
  
The technology has also changed the skills he looks for. Excel proficiency no longer carries the same weight it once did. He's hiring more people with strong communication skills, rather than technical ones, since AI tools can fill their knowledge gap. And job applicants can't be resistant to change.  
  
"I want problem solvers and I want critical thinkers," he said. "How can we use these tools today, knowing that the tools six months from now may be different, and that's okay?" [...] Educators say keeping pace with the technology's breakneck development will be a challenge, and are leaning more heavily on industry and employer feedback than ever before.  
  
"This kind of stuff literally changes over the weekend," said David Keathly, associate chair for undergraduate studies in UNT's computer science and engineering department.  
  
Eric Bergin, the founder of 3E Management, a Dallas private equity consulting company, is embedding the technology across his organization's workflow. Over the last three years, an in-house AI model has taken over junior analysts' grunt work, such as extracting data out of PDFs or modeling an acquisition.  
  
"We can do more work with less people," he said. [...] "We can do more work with less people," he said.  
  
Bergin expects new graduates to already be using AI tools in their everyday lives. What makes potential hires stand out is whether they know which platforms to use for which task — Claude for Excel, Gemini for research, ChatGPT for email-writing — and whether they can probe the answer that an AI model spits out.  
  
During the hiring process, Bergin asks applicants to model an example deal and send him an email write-up. He encourages them to use AI to complete the task, but will evaluate applicants based on whether they take
